description: Figure 2 is a regression-analysis plot illustrating the compositional influence on the elastic modulus of ferritic steels, where ferrite elemental concentrations and cementite contents were calculated with Thermo-Calc. The horizontal axis lists Fe, cementite, Cr, Mo, Si, Mn, and Cu in order of decreasing statistical significance (r_i/σ_i), and the vertical axis shows the regression coefficient r_i in GPa. Horizontal bars with error bars denote the fitted coefficients and their standard errors, while open circles represent literature data points. Si and Mn exhibit the strongest positive effects (approximately 5.5 and 5.4 GPa per mass%, respectively), followed by Mo (~3.9 GPa) and Cr (~2.2 GPa); Fe and cementite both lie near ~2.1 GPa. The caption reports that cementite, despite its statistical significance, has virtually no effect on the elastic modulus, and that the weak positive effect of Cu is insignificant owing to its large standard error. The accompanying text states the regression model achieves R² = 0.85 with a standard error of 1.1 GPa.
